prevalence.trend: stable; adult acne and psychosocial impact increasingly recognized

burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; moderate disability via QOL and mental health impact

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis; assess severity and scarring risk; evaluate for hyperandrogenism when indicated

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High to moderate; improved with guideline-based therapy and adherence

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care and dermatology pathways; specialist review for severe scarring or nodulocystic acne
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Topicals (benzoyl peroxide, retinoids), antibiotics when indicated, hormonal therapy (selected), isotretinoin with pregnancy prevention and monitoring
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Antibiotic stewardship and isotretinoin risk management; variable access to dermatology in some areas
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Broad access to dermatology and evidence-based therapies; structured isotretinoin monitoring programs
